threads.label: Iran / Hormuz
threads.status_line: Bürgenstock ceremony POSTPONED Jun 19 — Vance delayed, Iran citing Israel's Lebanon operations as precondition; Switzerland confirmed talks deferred; 60-day nuclear window started Jun 18 regardless; Hezbollah called deal 'great victory'; full MOU text not publicly released; Brent $78.72 (stable, deal priced in); Jun 22 talks still on calendar
expand_if: Iran sends delegation to Bürgenstock before Jun 22
Full MOU text released publicly
Jun 22 US-Iran talks collapse or produce agreement
Israeli strike in Lebanon after Jun 19 postponement
Brent snaps above $90 on deal fragility signal
Iran demands expand beyond Lebanon to other conditions
60-day window disputed by either side

threads.label: World Cup 2026
threads.status_line: Jun 19 fixtures: USA v Australia, Scotland v Morocco, Brazil v Haiti, Turkey v Paraguay; England next Jun 23 v Panama (after 4-2 Croatia win Jun 17, Kane brace = WC record equalled); Fleetwood Mac nostalgia wave tied to England mood + Stranger Things S5 effect continuing
expand_if: England vs Panama Jun 23
England vs Ghana Jun 27
Major injury to Kane or Bellingham
Major off-field controversy
Cultural flashpoint around tournament

threads.label: Ebola Bundibugyo — DRC / Uganda
threads.status_line: 837 confirmed / 196 deaths DRC as of Jun 17 (up from 808/192 on Jun 16); 29 new confirmed cases, 4 new deaths in latest report; Ituri epicentre 767 cases across 20 health zones; Uganda 19/2/no new cases since Jun 5; WHO PHEIC declared May 17; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; rate not declining
expand_if: Cases confirmed outside DRC/Uganda
DRC daily rate confirmed above 33 new cases/day sustained
WHO raises alert further
Vaccine supply or pipeline announcement
Confirmed case in Burundi, Rwanda, or South Sudan

threads.label: Israel / Lebanon
threads.status_line: Hezbollah chief Qassem called Geneva deal 'great victory,' linking it to Israeli withdrawal; Iran used Lebanon/Israel war to delay Bürgenstock formal talks Jun 19; Israel's Katz: will not withdraw from Lebanon; Jun 22 bilateral US-Iran talks still on calendar; Lebanon provisions of MOU still not publicly released; Iran-Hezbollah cash injection post-deal pending
expand_if: Major Israeli ground incursion or Hezbollah rocket barrage before Jun 22
Lebanon provisions of MOU revealed in full text
Jun 22 talks collapse or produce agreement on Lebanon
Iran cash injection to Hezbollah confirmed publicly
Hezbollah-Israel full ceasefire formally negotiated

threads.label: UK Labour Leadership
threads.status_line: Burnham WON Makerfield Jun 18-19; now in Parliament; formal challenge to Starmer expected within 48-72h; YouGov: Burnham national favourability -11 (most popular Labour politician but net negative); Reform leads Labour 42-34 nationally; 95+ MPs calling for Starmer departure; no credible centre-left alternative declared
expand_if: Burnham formally enters leadership race
Starmer resigns before challenge filed
Centre-left candidate emerges publicly
Major gilt/sterling reaction to political uncertainty
Starmer announces departure date

threads.label: SpaceX IPO / Musk Political Economy
threads.status_line: SPCX $185 Jun 19 (range $172-190); previous close $191.82; above $180 floor; +37% on $135 IPO close Jun 12; market cap ~$2.44T; profit-taking stabilising; no Musk political/regulatory development
expand_if: SPCX breaks below $180 (meaningful floor test)
Musk officially declared trillionaire
Starlink or xAI regulatory decision affected by political relationship
SpaceX/Musk-Trump relationship publicly strained or amplified
Musk makes significant public statement deviating from Trump administration line

threads.label: South Africa Xenophobia Crisis
threads.status_line: 586+ Nigerians repatriated; Malawians, Kenyans, Ghanaians in repatriation; Durban transit camps clearing; June 30 deadline 11 days away; cross-border trade -22%; justice minister acknowledged international damage; UN Secretary General and WHO chief condemned attacks
expand_if: June 30 deadline triggers mass violence
South African government formally criminalises or endorses movement
AU formal condemnation or UNHCR emergency declaration
Cross-border trade contraction exceeds 30%
Violence spreads to Johannesburg or Cape Town at scale
Malawi or Zimbabwe recalls ambassador

threads.label: UK Economic Stress
threads.status_line: FTSE ~10,510 (open Jun 19); Brent $78.72 (Iran deal relief trade holding); 10Y gilt 4.76% (easing); BoE holds rates Jun 19; political risk premium not yet priced into UK assets despite Labour leadership uncertainty; Iran deal 60-day window protecting energy-spike risk
expand_if: Gilt yield spike above 5.8% with upward trajectory
Energy price cap revision announced
Recession officially confirmed
Bank of England emergency action
Brent above $115 sustained
Iran deal collapses and Brent spikes back

threads.label: Colombia Runoff — Jun 21
threads.status_line: De la Espriella ~44% vs Cepeda ~41% first round; Trump endorsed De la Espriella; runoff Sunday Jun 21; tight race amid extreme polarisation; no major polling shift, violence, or candidate statement

Significant violence or electoral irregularity before Jun 21
Major polling shift
FARC or armed group statement on runoff

threads.label: European Defence / FCAS
threads.status_line: FCAS €100B sixth-gen fighter officially cancelled Jun 8-9; Combat Cloud survives; no new bilateral announcement or post-FCAS framework; European air power future unresolved
expand_if: France announces alternative bilateral fighter partnership
Germany announces F-35 or US-alternative procurement at scale
EU defence ministers joint communiqué on post-FCAS strategy
UK invited into new Franco-German defence cooperation framework

threads.label: Ukraine — Moscow drone assault
threads.status_line: Ukraine's largest-ever drone attack on Moscow Jun 17-18: 555+ drones, Kapotnya oil refinery hit twice, black smoke over capital; Russia claimed 200 drones destroyed on approach; TASS: 'most massive attack on Moscow region in two years'; Zelensky: 'time the war ended'; largely uncovered in UK press due to Iran deal dominating
expand_if: Russian retaliation against Ukrainian cities significantly escalates
Trump's Iran focus leads to US weapons supply delay for Ukraine
Zelensky proposes new ceasefire terms linked to Moscow strikes
Russia conducts comparable attack on Kyiv infrastructure
European NATO members respond to Ukraine drone capability demonstration

retired_threads.retired_because: Riots subsided; story fully absorbed into the Labour leadership contest. Burnham won Makerfield Jun 19; political fallout from riots now tracked under uk-labour-leadership. PSNI confirmed no paramilitary coordination.

source_discoveries.name: Richard Haass — 'Home & Away' Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former CFR president arguing Trump is recreating the JCPOA he destroyed, and that regime change pressure produced a more hardline Iranian government — structural counter-frame to deal optimism

source_discoveries.name: L'Orient Today
source_discoveries.why_interesting: English-language edition of L'Orient Le Jour; takes a meaningfully different political line from Al-Nahar on Lebanese politics; useful for south Lebanon coverage

source_discoveries.name: Asharq Al-Awsat (English)
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Gulf-backed pan-Arab daily; broke the Syria parliament June 8 story before other outlets; consistently ahead on Gulf/Arab political transitions

source_discoveries.name: Velina Tchakarova — Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Vienna-based geopolitical analyst; Austrian/Central European perspective on Ukraine distinct from Washington/London axis

source_discoveries.name: Alastair Crooke — Conflicts Forum Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former senior British diplomat and MI6 officer; argues Hezbollah advises Iran rather than takes orders; 'America's Suez Crisis' thesis; Jun 2026 prediction confirmed within 24h on Jun 11

source_discoveries.name: Trita Parsi — Quincy Institute / Responsible Statecraft
source_discoveries.why_interesting: NIAC founder, Quincy Institute fellow; consistent frame that Trump is not in a dominant position; Jun 12 'Warmongers in meltdown' piece names the Washington institutional battle most clearly

source_discoveries.name: Foundation for Defense of Democracies (FDD)
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Hawkish Washington think tank; building legal/institutional case for rejecting any Iran deal as insufficient; tracks closely with Netanyahu's likely response

source_discoveries.name: Hamidreza Azizi — SWP Berlin
source_discoveries.why_interesting: German-Iranian scholar at SWP; insider read on Iranian domestic discourse; cited by Fortune June 13 on birthday timing factor in deal delay

source_discoveries.name: Just Security
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Only serious legal/ethical analysis of the Khamenei assassination's precedent implications; argues deal's signing retroactively validates assassination as diplomatic strategy

source_discoveries.name: Carnegie Endowment — Emissary
source_discoveries.why_interesting: May 2026 piece argues Iran may quietly rebuild nuclear programme once Hormuz leverage is established — the deal defers rather than resolves the nuclear question

source_discoveries.name: Pan African Visions
source_discoveries.why_interesting: June 2026 structural analysis of South Africa xenophobia connecting the crisis to AfCFTA failure and ANC governance collapse; African-perspective framing entirely absent from Western coverage

source_discoveries.name: The Mekong Memo — August Whitcraft
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Substack covering Laos/Mekong region with granular depth; surfaced the Russia-Laos nuclear cooperation + OSJD rail corridor story — the parallel architecture Russia is building while Western attention is on Geneva

source_discoveries.name: Russia's Pivot to Asia
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Tracks Russia's systematic Rosatom nuclear client-building and infrastructure lock-in across the Global South; documents the pattern that the Iran deal does not reverse
